# netbox_available_ip (Resource)

Allocates the next available IP address from a given prefix.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Using prefix 1 for testing purposes. If it fails, I will change to another ID.
resource "netbox_available_ip" "test" {
  prefix_id   = 1
  description = "terraform test IP updated"
  status      = "active"
}

output "ip_address_output" {
  value = netbox_available_ip.test.ip_address
}

# Before running this, please create an IP range in your Netbox server
# and replace the `ip_range_id` below with the actual ID.
# Assuming range ID 1 for now.
resource "netbox_available_ip" "test_range_ip" {
  ip_range_id = 1
  status      = "active"
  description = "Allocated from terraform-created ip range"
}

output "allocated_ip_from_range" {
  value = netbox_available_ip.test_range_ip.ip_address
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `description` (String) Description for the IP address.
- `ip_range_id` (Number) The ID of the IP range to allocate the IP from. Exactly one of prefix_id or ip_range_id must be provided.
- `prefix_id` (Number) The ID of the prefix to allocate the IP from. Exactly one of prefix_id or ip_range_id must be provided.
- `status` (String) The status of the IP address (e.g., active, reserved).

### Read-Only

- `id` (Number) The numeric ID of the allocated IP address.
- `ip_address` (String) The allocated IP address (e.g., 192.168.1.10/24).
